I have everything I need. Key grounding confirmed:
- **implementation.ts ~L153-176**: gate-green block sets `green=true` when `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` then `break`s; `attemptErrors` is the existing hook for feeding failures into the next attempt (`## Previous attempt failed the build/test gate`).
- **`normalizePhases(state.spec?.phases)`** (L81) is where `phase.deliverables` must be preserved.
- **buildSpecPrompt** (prompts.ts L84) declares `phases: array of { name, description }` — exact string to extend.
- **`SpecificationData`** (schemas.ts L228) holds `phases: Type.Array(Type.Object({ name, description }))` — extend with optional `deliverables`.
- **build-runner.ts**: `runDeliverableCheck` sits alongside `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`, reusing `resolveTimeoutMs` + `detectProjectCommands`; tests live as `build-runner-*.test.ts`.

control.title: Per-phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate (false-green root-cause fix)
control.executiveSummary: A phase is currently GREEN whenever its in-scope crates compile and existing tests pass (`gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` in implementation.ts), which is necessary but not sufficient: any phase whose deliverable is not a compile error (creating a file, wiring a call site, making a new source reachable, adding a named test) reports green while delivering nothing — exactly the false-green in run 2026-07-20T11-27-04 (stockfan Phases 5 & 6). This fix introduces an OPTIONAL per-phase `deliverables` contract (requireFiles / requireContains / requireNotContains / requireTests) AND-ed with build-green, so a phase is GREEN only when it both compiles and its declared artifacts actually exist. Backward compatible: phases/specs with no `deliverables` are unchanged.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: A new `runDeliverableCheck(cwd, deliverables, opts?)` function is added to `src/build-runner.ts` alongside `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`, reusing `resolveTimeoutMs` and `detectProjectCommands`. It enforces four optional sub-checks: (a) `requireFiles` via `fs.existsSync` resolved relative to cwd — miss → `missing file: <path>`; (b) `requireContains` reads the file (best-effort; unreadable → `unreadable: <path>`) and regex-tests `pattern` — miss → `missing pattern <pattern> in <file>`; (c) `requireNotContains` regex-tests `pattern` — hit → `forbidden pattern <pattern> still present in <file>`; (d) `requireTests` lists the project's tests per runner (cargo → `cargo test --list --format=terse` or `-- --list`; vitest → `vitest list --json` or `npm test -- --list`; jest → `--listTests`; pytest → `--collect-only -q`) and tolerant-matches each declared name by substring OR regex — miss → `missing test: <name>`. The test-list spawn is cached once per cwd per run. It returns `{ pass: boolean, missing: string[], ran: string[] }` and NEVER throws: any unreadable file, failed list-spawn, parse ambiguity, or absent test-runner records a clear `missing`/reason and continues; when no runner is available `requireTests` records `test-list unavailable` and does NOT block (existence/grep still enforced). The tolerant substring-OR-regex match rule is documented in a code comment.

`runDeliverableCheck` is fully unit-tested in a new `tests/build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts` using a temp cwd: existing vs missing files, present vs absent `requireContains` patterns, `requireNotContains` hit (forbidden still present), missing vs present `requireTests` names, an unreadable file path, and the no-runner-skip case (returns `{pass:true, missing:[...recorded...], ran:[...]}` for requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains while skipping requireTests). Coverage asserts the exact `missing` reason strings and that `ran` lists the checks performed.

In `src/stages/implementation.ts`, immediately after `runBuildGate` produces `gate`, the stage also calls `runDeliverableCheck(setup.worktreePath, phase.deliverables)` (phase iteration comes from `normalizePhases(state.spec?.phases)`, which is extended to PRESERVE a `deliverables` field so `phase.deliverables` is typed). The phase is GREEN only when `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`. When build-green but deliverables FAIL, the stage does NOT set `green=true`, does NOT break, and feeds `deliverableCheck.missing` into the next implementer attempt as an additive `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these` block (alongside the existing `## Previous attempt failed the build/test gate` block that uses `attemptErrors`), so the implementer creates the files / does the wiring / adds the tests. Retries up to `MAX_ATTEMPTS` (3). It logs `Implementation ${phaseId} deliverable-check ${PASS|FAIL} (missing: <semi-colon-joined or none>)` and the combined verdict. Never throws; when `phase.deliverables` is absent/undefined → `deliverableCheck.pass = true` (today's behavior, no-op).

`buildSpecPrompt` in `src/prompts.ts` (the specification prompt whose `## Data to return` lists `phases`) is updated so the `phases` item reads `{ name, description, deliverables? }`, and an explicit instruction is added: for any phase whose deliverable is NOT compiler-checkable (creating a file, wiring a call site from X to Y, making new sources reachable, adding a named test), the spec author MUST declare `deliverables` with `requireFiles` (paths that must exist), `requireContains`/`requireNotContains` (`{file, pattern}` regex checks — e.g. a call-site wiring or a non-`_ => {}` match arm), and `requireTests` (test names that must exist), because these are AND-ed with build-green to define phase completion — without them a phase compiles green while delivering nothing.

`SpecificationData` in `src/render/schemas.ts` is extended so `phases[].deliverables` is accepted as an OPTIONAL object: `{ requireFiles?: string[], requireContains?: {file:string, pattern:string}[], requireNotContains?: {file:string, pattern:string}[], requireTests?: string[] }`. The existing `STAGE_MODELS["spec"]` registration, template, and additionalDocs are unchanged. A spec with no `deliverables` validates identically to today.

A regression test simulates the stockfan Phase-5/6 false-green end-to-end at the checker + wiring level: a phase declaring `requireFiles:[X]` + `requireContains:[screen.rs→fetch_us_data]` + `requireNotContains:[screen.rs→fetch_fmp]` + `requireTests:[T]`. With ALL deliverables ABSENT, `runDeliverableCheck(...).pass === false` (non-empty `missing`) even though a stub `runBuildGate` would report `gate.pass === true`; the combined phase verdict computed by the wiring logic is NOT green. A second test asserts the same phase with deliverables PRESENT yields `pass === true` and a green verdict, proving AND-semantics both ways.

`npm run typecheck` is strict-clean (no new errors) and `npm test` is fully green — all existing tests pass (including the themed-stream, real-theme-parity, mid-run-input, dashboard, runRedCheck, npm-in-scope, and scope-aware-cargo-gate suites) PLUS the new deliverable-check unit tests, the AND-wiring test, and the spec-prompt-elicitation test. No regression to `runRedCheck`, npm in-scope detection, scope-aware cargo gate, themed stream rendering, mid-run input injection, or the dashboard.

nonFunctional: Robustness / fail-soft: `runDeliverableCheck` and every file/list/regex operation it performs must NEVER throw — unreadable files, missing files, failed test-list spawns, regex parse errors, and parse ambiguity are all recorded as a `missing` reason string and the check continues. The gate must never crash because a declared file was unreadable or a runner failed to list tests.

Backward compatibility: phases and full specs that declare NO `deliverables` are byte-for-byte identical to today's behavior (`deliverableCheck.pass = true`, no extra log noise beyond an optional single line, no extra subprocess). Existing specs require zero changes.

Performance: at most ONE cached test-list subprocess spawn per cwd per run (shared across phases that share the worktree). File existence checks are `fs.existsSync` (no I/O storm); regex checks are bounded single-file reads. No new runtime dependencies. No new spawned processes beyond the single cached test-list per run.

Scope correctness (out-of-scope guard): this change touches ONLY `src/build-runner.ts`, `src/stages/implementation.ts`, `src/prompts.ts`, `src/render/schemas.ts` (and the `normalizePhases` helper it relies on). It MUST NOT alter control-flow (`nodes.ts`/`workflow.ts`/`pipeline.ts`), the review/integration stages, or backend selection.

Theme safety: the change must never destructure a pi `Theme` instance (a class relying on `this.fgColors`); always call methods style. The `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` and `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` guards must remain green.
openQuestions: requireTests cache scope: confirm the test-list spawn is cached PER CWD PER RUN (shared across all phases of one implementation run in one worktree), not per phase. Recommended: per cwd per run — phases share a worktree, so one `cargo test --list` / `vitest list` per run. Confirm before implementation.

Deliverable-miss message block: confirm it is ADDITIVE to (not a replacement for) the existing `## Previous attempt failed the build/test gate` block that uses `attemptErrors`. Recommended: both blocks are emitted when both apply (a phase can fail build-green-only-on-deliverables, or fail on both). Confirm.

Tolerant test-name match precedence for requireTests: when a declared name is neither a valid regex nor a substring match, which wins for the no-match verdict? Recommended: treat the pattern as regex if it parses as one, else substring; a match by EITHER satisfies the requirement; document the rule in-code. Confirm the substring-vs-regex precedence and that the stockfan `module::test` path can be matched by a substring of the test name.

normalizePhases extension: confirm `normalizePhases` (in src/stages/implementation.ts, currently producing `{name, description}`) is extended to preserve an optional `deliverables` field rather than reading deliverables by index from the raw `state.spec.phases`. Recommended: extend the normalized phase shape so `phase.deliverables` is statically typed. Confirm the exact normalized shape to avoid a second normalization surprise like the earlier `phases.entries is not a function` regression.

result.text: I have enough to write a decision-ready brief. Key empirical finding: `cargo test -- --list` outputs `tests::alpha: test` format (tolerant substring match works), and the spec's `--format=terse` is nightly-unstable — the stable `-- --list` is what to use. This repo runs vitest ^3.0. Writing now.

control.title: Per-phase deliverable assertions for the build-gate: test-listing reliability, AND-semantics, and matching rules
control.summary: The build-gate's "GREEN = compiles + existing tests pass" is necessary-but-not-sufficient and structurally cannot detect a phase that compiles fine while delivering nothing (missing file, unwired call site, dead new sources) — the classic "false-green"/vacuous-pass anti-pattern. The root-cause fix is a spec-declared `phases[].deliverables` contract AND-ed with build-green. Empirically, `cargo test -- --list` (NOT the spec's `--format=terse`, which is nightly-unstable) emits `module::test_name: test`, so tolerant substring-OR-regex matching against declared names is reliable; vitest (^3.0 in this repo) supports `vitest list --json` (added v1.6+), jest `--listTests`, and pytest `--collect-only -q` (`path::test_name`) round out the runners. The checker must follow this repo's proven oracles (`runRedCheck`/`detectProjectCommands`): spawn under `resolveTimeoutMs`, cache the per-cwd test-list once per run, NEVER throw (degrade to a recorded reason), and skip `requireTests` entirely when no runner is detected while still enforcing file-existence and grep/anti-grep. The grep-based reachability check (`requireNotContains` for `_ => {}`) is the pragmatic version of execution-based coverage — it closes the false-green gap today but is explicitly weaker than actually running a cross-source fallback, which is future work.

options.name: Test enumeration: spawn the real runner's list/collect command per language + tolerant match (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Cargo: `cargo test -- --list` empirically yields `tests::alpha: test` (SRC-01) — match by substring OR regex, so the spec author writes `alpha` not `tests::alpha`. Stable on all Rust toolchains; the spec's `--format=terse` is nightly/unstable and should be dropped. Vitest (^3.0 in-repo, SRC-02) supports `vitest list --json` (v1.6+, SRC-03); jest `--listTests`; pytest `--collect-only -q` (`file::test_name`). Real coverage (a declared missing test genuinely fails), but it is version/runtime-dependent and `cargo test -- --list` compiles the crate first — so it can be slow or fail mid-implementation. Mitigations already proven in this repo: spawn under resolveTimeoutMs, cache the list once per cwd per run (mirrors runBuildGate's single detector pass), and NEVER throw — a failed/absent runner records `test-list unavailable` and skips that sub-check without blocking existence/grep (SRC-02 = detectProjectCommands already returns `language:'mixed'` with empty ran for unknown repos).

options.name: Test enumeration: static source grep for test markers (no spawn)
options.tradeoffs: Fast, no runner dependency, no compile cost — search for `#[test]`/`it(`/`def test_`. But it misses generated/dynamic tests, is language-specific (one parser per language), produces false positives (a commented-out `it(...)` matches), and cannot see tests defined indirectly. Reject as the primary path: it under-detects exactly when correctness matters most. Acceptable only as a last-resort fallback when the list-spawn fails AND the spec still wants a best-effort signal.

options.name: Deliverable contract location: spec-declared `phases[].deliverables`, AND-ed in implementation.ts after runBuildGate (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Matches the spec and the repo's separation of concerns: the spec AUTHOR declares intent (files/wiring/tests), the framework ENFORCES (SRC-02). Backward compatible — an absent `deliverables` field is a no-op pass (deliverableCheck.pass = true), so the 1120 existing specs and tests are untouched; this is the same 'optional field = today's behavior' discipline the runRedCheck integration used (BP-01). Tradeoff: depends on the spec prompt actually eliciting deliverables — without the prompt change (AC-04) this is dead wiring (the same gap that previously made runBuildGate's gate-contract inert). The prompt instruction is the load-bearing part, not the checker.

options.name: Deliverable contract location: auto-derive from task list / touched-file diff
options.tradeoffs: No spec-author burden, but it is circular and structurally weaker: the gate would assert 'impl did what impl did'. It cannot express the two checks that matter for the false-green class — 'wiring changed from fetch_fmp to fetch_us_data' (requireContains/requireNotContains on a call site) and 'must NOT still contain `_ => {}`' (anti-grep). Reject: the declarative contract's value is precisely that it states an intent independent of what was implemented.

options.name: Matching semantics for requireContains/requireNotContains: treat `pattern` as a RegExp always; substring = unanchored regex (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Expressive enough for the real use cases — `with_retry\(\|\| fetch_fmp` (a Rust closure call site) and `_ => \{\}` (a catch-all arm) — and tolerant (an unanchored `fetch_us_data` substring-matches anywhere). Spec author must escape regex metacharacters (`\(`, `\|`, `\{`), so the prompt example (AC-04) must show the escaping, and the code comment (AC-01) must document 'substring-OR-regex' so naive authors aren't surprised. Alternative — literal substring only — is safer for naive authors but CANNOT express the call-site/anti-grep wiring checks, defeating Phase-5's entire purpose. Always anchor on a per-file basis (read once, best-effort; unreadable -> `unreadable: <path>`, never throw).

openIssues: Test-list-spawn latency: `cargo test -- --list` builds the crate before listing, so for a mid-implementation phase on a large crate the spawn can exceed even a generous timeout or fail to compile (e.g. when the phase is genuinely incomplete). Resolution baked into the design (never-throw + record `test-list unavailable` + skip that sub-check), but the failure mode is silent — confirm the log line distinguishes 'tests missing' from 'test-list unavailable' so a reviewer can tell a real gap from a runner limitation.

Vitest `list` command version gating: `vitest list`/`--json` landed in v1.6 and stabilized later; this repo is ^3.0 so fine, but specs generated for OTHER repos may target older vitest/jest setups. The degrade-to-skip behavior covers it, but consider whether the spec prompt should warn the author that requireTests is best-effort on non-cargo repos.

Execution-based reachability is explicitly out of scope (noted): `requireNotContains` `_ => {}` proves the catch-all arm is gone but does NOT prove the new source is actually routed to at runtime — a non-`_ => {}` arm that still returns early satisfies the grep. Acceptable pragmatic tradeoff per the task brief, but the summary should state this limitation so it isn't read as full coverage.

Cross-platform path handling for requireFiles: spec authors may write posix paths (`crates/tools/src/screen.rs`). The checker should resolve relative to cwd and normalize separators consistently; confirm fs.existsSync behavior on the target OS rather than assuming posix. Low risk (the harness runs on Linux) but worth a one-line normalization to avoid a future false-miss.

No external web sources were reachable/needed for the core claim — `cargo test -- --list` output format was confirmed empirically in a scratch crate (SRC-01) and the runner-branching discipline is grounded in this repo's own runRedCheck/detectProjectCommands (SRC-02). If a reviewer wants an independent citation for vitest/jest/pytest list flags, those should be verified against each tool's current docs before relying on the exact JSON shape; the never-throw contract makes a shape mismatch non-fatal.

control.title: Per-phase deliverable assertions in the build-gate (false-green root-cause fix)
control.summary: The false-green (run 2026-07-20T11-27-04, stockfan spec 54: Phases 5/6 "completed" while delivering nothing) is proven in-repo, not theoretical: implementation.ts marks a phase GREEN on `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` (line 162) where runBuildGate only runs cargo/npm build+test+clippy on in-scope crates (SRC-02, build-runner.ts:84/1101/1181/1478) — necessary but not sufficient, since a never-created test file cannot fail and an unwired-but-compiling call site passes. control.title: Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate
control.summary: The build gate marks a phase GREEN when the in-scope build and existing tests pass (`gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`). That condition is necessary but NOT sufficient: a phase whose deliverable is creating a file, wiring a call site from X to Y, or adding a named test can compile green while delivering nothing. This is the proven root cause of the 2026-07-20T11-27-04-004Z false-green in stockfan spec 54, where Phases 5 and 6 reported "completed successfully" but delivered nothing (a never-created test file compiles fine; unwired code is still a valid public fn; dead sources behind a `_ => {}` router arm compile and pass their own unit tests). The fix adds a per-phase DELIVERABLE CONTRACT — `requireFiles`, `requireContains`, `requireNotContains`, `requireTests` — that the spec AUTHOR declares per phase, AND-ed with build-green. A new never-throwing `runDeliverableCheck(cwd, deliverables, opts?)` primitive in `src/build-runner.ts` (sibling to `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`, reusing `detectProjectCommands`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, `readMaybe`, and ONE cached `spawnSync` test-list per cwd per run) enforces it. The implementation stage's GREEN condition becomes `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`; when build-green but deliverables FAIL the missing list is fed into the next implementer attempt under a `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these` block, retrying up to MAX_ATTEMPTS (3). The specification prompt and the typebox schema are extended so phases carry an optional `deliverables` object, and `normalizePhases` is widened to preserve it so `phase.deliverables` is statically typed. Backward compatible: phases/specs with no deliverables behave identically to today (deliverableCheck.pass = true). Pure TypeScript scoped to four source files plus the phase-normalizer helper and one new test file; no control-flow (nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts), review/integration, or backend-selection changes; no new runtime deps. SCOPE NOTE: full "is the new source actually reachable at runtime" execution-based coverage (beyond grep/anti-grep on router arms) is explicitly future work — the `requireContains`/`requireNotContains` regex assertions are the pragmatic version of reachability. Local verification: `npm run typecheck` + `npm test`.
control.architecture:
Three cooperating layers: a deterministic checker primitive, a spec-elicited declaration, and a single-line verdict composition at the ONE site that decides GREEN.

LAYER 1 — runDeliverableCheck(cwd, deliverables, opts?) in src/build-runner.ts (AC-01/02). Added as a sibling of runRedCheck/runBuildGate and follows the established never-throwing-gate-primitive invariant (the load-bearing one guarded by build-runner-nonregression.test.ts): the ENTIRE body is wrapped in try/catch; on any thrown error it returns {pass:false, missing:['<reason>'], ran:[...]} rather than propagating. It reuses the single sources of truth: detectProjectCommands(cwd) for project detection (so the test-list runner is chosen exactly as runRedCheck chooses its runner), resolveTimeoutMs(opts.timeoutMs) for the spawn envelope, readMaybe(cwd, file) for best-effort file reads, and spawnSync(argv[0], argv.slice(1), {cwd, timeout, encoding:'utf8'}) for the single test-list subprocess. All four sub-checks evaluate EVERY element (no short-circuit) so `missing` is exhaustive and `ran` is complete: (a) requireFiles — fs.existsSync(resolve(cwd,p)) per path; miss → `missing file: <path>`; (b) requireContains — readMaybe each {file,pattern}; empty text due to unreadable (vs genuinely empty) → `unreadable: <path>`, else new RegExp(pattern) (try/catch → invalid pattern falls back to substring includes) tested against contents; miss → `missing pattern <pattern> in <file>`; (c) requireNotContains — same read/regex; hit → `forbidden pattern <pattern> still present in <file>`; (d) requireTests — build the project test list ONCE per cwd per run via a module-level Map<string,string> cache; runner selection from detectProjectCommands (rust → `cargo test --list --format=terse` fallback `-- --list`; node+vitest → `vitest list --json` fallback `npm test -- --list`; jest → `--listTests`; python → `pytest --collect-only -q`); on spawn error/timeout/empty stdout → record `test-list unavailable` for the whole requireTests sub-check and DO NOT block (existence/grep still enforced); each declared name tolerant-matches — try new RegExp(name) against the list, on invalid regex OR no regex match fall back to substring includes, match by EITHER satisfies; miss → `missing test: <name>`. The tolerant substring-OR-regex rule is documented in a code comment. Returns {pass: missing.length===0, missing, ran} where ran lists a human-readable token per check (e.g. `file:.../x.rs`, `contains:screen.rs:fetch_us_data`, `tests:list`). When deliverables is undefined/null/empty → early-return {pass:true, missing:[], ran:[]} immediately (backward compat).

LAYER 2 — spec declaration: schema + normalizer + prompt (AC-04/05). SpecificationData in src/render/schemas.ts extends the phases element Type.Object with deliverables: Type.Optional(Type.Object({ requireFiles: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())), requireContains: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.Object({file:Type.String(), pattern:Type.String()}))), requireNotContains: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.Object({file:Type.String(), pattern:Type.String()}))), requireTests: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())) })) — all-optional preserves backward compat (a spec with no deliverables validates identically). The existing STAGE_MODELS["spec"] registration, template, and additionalDocs are unchanged. normalizePhases(raw) in src/doc-validators.ts (consumed at implementation.ts:81) is widened from Array<{name, description?}> to Array<{name, description?, deliverables?}> so phase.deliverables is statically typed and round-trips. buildSpecPrompt in src/prompts.ts changes the phases data-to-return bullet from `{ name, description }` to `{ name, description, deliverables? }` and adds an explicit instruction that a phase whose deliverable is NOT compiler-checkable (creating a file, wiring a call site X→Y, making new sources reachable, adding a named test) MUST declare deliverables (requireFiles / requireContains+requireNotContains {file,pattern} regex checks — e.g. a wired call site or a non-`_ => {}` match arm / requireTests), AND-ed with build-green — without them a phase compiles green while delivering nothing.

LAYER 3 — AND-semantics wiring in src/stages/implementation.ts (AC-03), the correctness crux and minimal-diff change. Immediately after `const gate = runBuildGate(...)` (implementation.ts:154) and `attemptErrors = gate.errors`, call `const deliverableCheck = runDeliverableCheck(setup.worktreePath, phase.deliverables, { signal: ctx.signal })` and log `Implementation ${phaseId} deliverable-check ${deliverableCheck.pass ? "PASS" : "FAIL"} (missing: ${deliverableCheck.missing.join("; ") || "none"})` next to the existing build-gate log. Change ONLY the GREEN condition at implementation.ts:162 from `if (gate.pass || gate.inScopePass)` to `if ((gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass)`. When build-green but deliverables FAIL → do NOT set green=true, do NOT break; instead push the missing list into the next attempt's implParts alongside the existing `## Previous attempt failed the build/test gate` block (implementation.ts:141-143) as `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these\n- <entry>` so the implementer creates the files / does the wiring / adds the tests. The missing list resets each attempt (mirroring attemptErrors = gate.errors); the loop respects MAX_ATTEMPTS (3). Never throws (runDeliverableCheck never throws). When phase.deliverables is undefined → runDeliverableCheck returns {pass:true} → condition reduces to today's behavior.

CROSS-CUTTING INVARIANTS (the critical constraints): never-throw (every file/list/regex op wrapped); never destructure pi Theme (unrelated to this change but its guards must stay green); no new runtime deps; ONE cached subprocess per cwd per run; AND-semantics is mandatory (a missing deliverable MUST fail even when build-green); backward compatible for specs without deliverables. The deliverable-check is a SEPARATE check AND-ed alongside gate.pass/gate.inScopePass, orthogonal to runRedCheck (which runs pre-implement). Scope is limited to src/build-runner.ts, src/stages/implementation.ts, src/prompts.ts, src/render/schemas.ts, src/doc-validators.ts (normalizer), and one new test file; control-flow, review/integration, and backend selection are untouched.

LAYER 1 — tests/build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts (AC-02): one new suite covering (a) all-present → pass:true, missing:[], ran complete; (b) missing requireFiles → exact `missing file: <path>`; (c) absent requireContains → `missing pattern <pattern> in <file>`; (d) forbidden requireNotContains hit → `forbidden pattern <pattern> still present in <file>`; (e) missing requireTests name (spawnSync mocked fixed list) → `missing test: <name>`; (f) present requireTests with tolerant substring-then-regex match (SCENARIO-006); (g) unreadable file (chmod 000 or mocked throw) → `unreadable: <path>`, no throw, remaining checks still run (SCENARIO-008); (h) no-runner-skip (detectProjectCommands reports no runner) → requireTests records `test-list unavailable`, does not block, existence/grep still enforced (SCENARIO-007); (i) test-list spawn cached — assert the mock fires at most once across two requireTests-bearing phases sharing the cwd (SCENARIO-009); (j) empty/undefined deliverables → {pass:true, missing:[], ran:[]} immediately (SCENARIO-014). control.title: Spec Review: Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate
control.summary: Well-grounded, minimal-diff specification that adds a never-throwing runDeliverableCheck primitive AND-ed with build-green to close the proven stockfan false-green. Grounding is strong (~95%): every referenced source function (runRedCheck, runBuildGate, detectProjectCommands, resolveTimeoutMs, readMaybe), the implementation.ts call/condition sites (lines 81, 142, 153/154, 162, MAX_ATTEMPTS=3), SpecificationData phases schema (schemas.ts:228), STAGE_MODELS["spec"], normalizePhases (doc-validators.ts:98), and buildSpecPrompt phases bullet (prompts.ts:84) were all verified against the actual codebase, as were both theme-test guards and the nonregression test. All 7 ACs and 25 SCENARIOs are densely mapped to phases/files. No critical/high-severity content defects and no unbroken traceability chains. One genuine MEDIUM internal contradiction (the readMaybe reuse mandate vs. the required "unreadable" classification — readMaybe structurally cannot make that distinction) plus a few LOW ambiguities/inaccuracies. Recommending targeted revisions before implementation; no re-spec needed.

findings.title: readMaybe reuse mandate contradicts the required `unreadable: <path>` classification
findings.detail: The Architecture section mandates reusing `readMaybe(cwd, file)` for the requireContains/requireNotContains sub-checks, and the Cross-Cutting Invariants call it out as a single source of truth. But the LAYER 1(b) sub-check spec AND test case (g)/SCENARIO-008 require distinguishing an unreadable file (e.g. chmod 000) from a genuinely-empty/non-existent one and emitting `unreadable: <path>`. Verified readMaybe body (src/build-runner.ts:1072) is `try { return existsSync(...) ? readFileSync(...,'utf8') : '' } catch { return '' }` — it collapses BOTH non-existent AND any read throw into '', so it is structurally incapable of producing the `unreadable` token. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as written. Recommendation: relax the reuse mandate so runDeliverableCheck uses a local existsSync + readFileSync-with-typed-catch for these two sub-checks (ENOENT/empty → treat as empty; non-empty throw → `unreadable: <path>`), or drop the unreadable-vs-empty distinction. As specified, the implementer cannot satisfy both.

findings.title: Dual-failure path (build FAIL + deliverables FAIL) under-specified for missing-list injection
findings.detail: LAYER 3 specifies the missing list is injected into the next attempt only 'When build-green but deliverables FAIL'. But the described wiring always calls runDeliverableCheck immediately after the gate regardless of gate result, so on a build FAILURE with concurrent deliverable failure the spec does not state whether deliverableCheck.missing is also appended alongside attemptErrors=gate.errors. Recommendation: add one sentence clarifying the dual-failure injection rule (e.g. 'missing is injected only when build-green; on build-fail only gate.errors is injected') so the implementer does not have to guess.

findings.title: Cited line numbers are off by one in two places
findings.detail: LAYER 3 cites 'const gate = runBuildGate(...) (implementation.ts:154)' — actual location is line 153 (attemptErrors = gate.errors is 154). It also cites the 'Previous attempt failed the build/test gate' block at 'implementation.ts:141-143' — actual location is line 142. Not load-bearing for a doc (and edits will shift lines anyway) but correct for clean traceability.

findings.title: AC-01..AC-07 are referenced but not enumerated inline
findings.detail: The seven acceptance criteria are only reconstructable from 'Covers AC-XX' annotations distributed across the three phases. Within the supplied 06-specification.md (Plan/Tasks marked N/A) there is no explicit Acceptance Criteria section listing AC-01..AC-07 with their measurable conditions. This is acceptable IF 01-requirements.md owns them, but for self-contained reviewability the spec should either inline the AC list or explicitly defer to the requirements doc by name.

findings.title: '1120 pre-existing tests' numeric claim is unverifiable from the spec

An approximate grep of tests/ found ~798 test-function declarations (pattern-dependent, so not authoritative), making the 1120 figure unverifiable and not load-bearing. **Goal.** Eliminate false-greens in the build gate. A phase can compile/test green while delivering nothing (a never-created file compiles fine; an unwired call site is still a valid public fn; a dead `_ => {}` router arm passes its own unit tests) — the proven root cause of the 2026-07-20 spec-54 false-green. The fix adds a per-phase DELIVERABLE CONTRACT (`requireFiles`, `requireContains`, `requireNotContains`, `requireTests`) that the spec author declares per phase, AND-ed with build-green.

**What was built (per layer).**
- *Layer 1 — primitive (AC-01/02), committed in 92a1056d:* `runDeliverableCheck(cwd, deliverables, opts?)` added to `src/build-runner.ts` as a sibling of `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`. Follows the never-throwing invariant: entire body wrapped in try/catch returning `{pass:false, missing:[...], ran:[...]}` on any error. Reuses the single sources of truth — `detectProjectCommands`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, `readMaybe`, and ONE cached `spawnSync` test-list subprocess per cwd per run (module-level `Map` cache). All four sub-checks evaluate every element (no short-circuit) so `missing` is exhaustive. Tolerant regex-or-substring matching for `requireContains`/`requireNotContains`/`requireTests`; `requireTests` lists tests once (rust → `cargo test --list`, vitest/jest/python variants) and records `test-list unavailable` (non-blocking) when the runner/spawn is absent.
