control.title: Adversarial Review — Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions (build-gate deliverable contract)
control.summary:
The implementation is mostly well-built: the never-throw invariant is genuinely enforced (whole-body try/catch, exhaustive sub-check evaluation, tolerant substring-OR-regex matching), AND-semantics is wired at the single correct site (implementation.ts:184 `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`), backward-compat early-returns {pass:true} for absent deliverables, and the schema/normalizer/prompt layer is consistently extended. The primitive unit suite (build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts) is thorough on the checker in isolation.

However, the review found one HIGH-severity correctness defect that directly defeats the feature's stated purpose and the regression it was built to prevent. The module-level `testListCache` (build-runner.ts:1629) is keyed by absolute `cwd` with NO invalidation. It is seeded on the first `runDeliverableCheck` call for a cwd and never refreshed — while `runDeliverableCheck` is invoked once per implementation ATTEMPT and once per PHASE on the SAME worktreePath. Because the test list is mutated by the implementation itself between those calls, a stale snapshot causes `requireTests` to report a freshly-created test as forever-missing: (a) within a phase, if attempt 1's implementer fails to add test T, the cache is seeded without T, and attempts 2–3 that correctly create T still read the stale list → phase fails despite delivering; (b) across phases, the first requireTests-bearing phase seeds the cache for the whole run, so any LATER phase whose requireTests test is created during that phase is unsatisfiable. requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains correctly re-read from disk each attempt, but requireTests is pinned to the first snapshot. This is a deterministic false-NEGATIVE (green-delivered phase reported not-green → early termination, allGreen=false) for exactly the multi-phase "add a named test" specs the feature targets — including the stockfan Phase-6 shape in AC-04/AC-06. SCENARIO-009 only asserts the lister spawns once; it never exercises the retry-then-create-then-pass path, so the bug is latent and uncovered (and the test actively locks in the buggy invariant). Because this will cause production pipeline failures on the feature's primary use case, the verdict is REJECT. Medium/low findings (silent requireNotContains pass on missing file; dead `ran` field never logged; unbounded process-global cache with no reset hook; deliverable-check lister spawn running even when build-gate already failed and seeding a poisoned cache) are documented below but are not REJECT drivers.

findings.title: Stale module-level test-list cache makes requireTests false-negative across retries and across phases
findings.detail: `testListCache` is a process-global `Map<string,TestListResult>` keyed by absolute cwd with no invalidation (grep confirms only .get and two .set sites; no .delete/.clear/reset). `runDeliverableCheck` is called once per implementation attempt (implementation.ts:175) AND once per phase, all on the same `setup.worktreePath`. The first call seeds the cache; every later call returns that snapshot. Since the implementer CREATES tests between calls, the cache is stale by construction. Two failure modes: (a) within-phase retry — attempt 1 fails to add T, lister seeds cache without T; attempt 2 creates T correctly but deliverableCheck returns the stale list → still 'missing test: T' → MAX_ATTEMPTS exhausted → phase fails despite delivering. (b) cross-phase — Phase 1 (requireTests:[T1]) seeds the cache for the whole run; Phase N (requireTests:[TN], TN created in Phase N) reads Phase-1's snapshot → TN forever missing. requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains re-read disk each attempt so they recover across retries; requireTests does not. This breaks the stockfan AC-04/AC-06 regression shape (a requireTests entry for a test that must be CREATED) and any multi-phase spec where each phase adds a named test. Fix: invalidate per-attempt (key by cwd+attempt token, clear after each attempt, or re-spawn when a prior call for this cwd reported a missing test) — or simply do not cache across the per-phase retry loop. The SCENARIO-009 test asserts 'spawns at most once per cwd', which is the buggy invariant; it must instead assert per-attempt or post-mutation re-spawn.

findings.title: requireNotContains silently PASSES when the target file is missing or unreadable
findings.detail: For requireNotContains, when readForDeliverable returns !rd.ok (file missing OR unreadable), the loop emits NO missing entry — the check trivially passes. Under AND-semantics this means: if the implementer DELETES the file that was supposed to have the forbidden pattern removed, the phase goes green. The stockfan pairing masks this (requireContains on the same screen.rs would also fail), but a LONE requireNotContains on a missing/unreadable file is a silent pass with no signal in `missing` or `ran` distinguishing 'forbidden pattern absent because file is healthy' from 'forbidden pattern absent because file vanished'. Recommend either recording a 'not-contains:file-missing:<file>' note in ran, or documenting that requireNotContains MUST be paired with requireFiles/requireContains for soundness.

findings.title: `ran` field is dead output — computed on every sub-check, never consumed or logged
findings.detail: grep confirms `deliverableCheck.ran` is never read in production code. implementation.ts logs only `deliverableCheck.pass` and `deliverableCheck.missing.join('; ')` — it never logs `.ran`, unlike the sibling build-gate log on line 167 which logs `gate.ran.join(', ')`. Every other gate's `.ran` IS surfaced (verify.ts:88, stages/index.ts:54). So `runDeliverableCheck` allocates ~4+ ran-tokens per call for zero readers. Either add parity logging (`(ran: ${deliverableCheck.ran.join(', ')})`) so the audit trail is actually auditable, or drop the field. As shipped it is spec-mandated but cargo-culted dead data.

findings.title: testListCache is unbounded process-global state with no run-boundary reset hook
findings.detail: The cache lives at module scope with no exported clear() and no eviction. The comment claims 'vi.resetModules() (or process exit) clears it' — true for the vitest process model, but if super-dev is ever invoked as a library, embedded in a long-lived process, or run in watch mode, stale lists from a PRIOR pipeline run leak into the next run (interacting with F1 to amplify false-negatives across runs). Recommend exporting a `clearDeliverableCaches()` called at run start, or scoping the cache to a per-run object rather than module-global. Low severity for the current one-CLI-process-per-run model but a latent footgun.

findings.title: Deliverable-check (and its test-lister spawn) runs even when the build-gate already FAILED, seeding a poisoned cache
findings.detail: runDeliverableCheck is invoked unconditionally after runBuildGate, including when gate.pass===false && inScopePass===false. On a broken build the `cargo test --list` / `vitest list` spawn may itself fail or return a partial/empty list, which (per F1) then poisons the cache for all subsequent attempts. Consider gating the requireTests sub-check (or the whole deliverable-check) behind `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`, or clearing the cache entry when the spawn occurs on a build-broken attempt. Minor cost today, but it is the cache-poisoning on-ramp for F1.

findings.title: SCENARIO-009 test asserts the stale-cache behavior as if it were correct
findings.detail: The cache test asserts the lister 'spawns at most once across two requireTests calls sharing a cwd' and treats that as the desired invariant. That is precisely the behavior that causes F1. The test should be reframed to assert 'at most one spawn per attempt' or 're-spawns after the worktree/test-set changes', and should add a retry-then-create-then-PASS case (call with test absent → cache seeded; create test on disk; call again → pass) which would currently fail and expose the bug. As written, the suite gives false confidence that the caching is sound.

result.text:
I have enough to render the review. Key findings confirmed:

1. **HIGH — testListCache staleness**: The module-level `testListCache` (build-runner.ts:1629) is never invalidated (only `.get`/`.set`, no `.delete`/`.clear`, confirmed by grep). `loadTestList` returns a cache hit before re-spawning. This is correct for the cross-PHASE case (SCENARIO-009 asserts it), but within a single phase's retry loop, attempt 1 caches the test list *without* a declared test; when the implementer creates that test on attempt 2 (guided by the "Deliverables still missing" block), `runDeliverableCheck` still reads the stale cache → `requireTests` keeps failing → phase aborts after MAX_ATTEMPTS despite the test now existing. This directly defeats the documented recovery mechanism and produces a false-NEGATIVE (aborts a run that actually succeeded).

2. The other 3 deliverable types (requireFiles/Contains/NotContains) re-read the filesystem each call and are correct.

Typecheck is clean; 53 new tests pass.

control.title: Code Review: Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate
control.summary: The implementation delivers the per-phase deliverable contract end-to-end: `runDeliverableCheck` (src/build-runner.ts) is a never-throwing sibling oracle implementing requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains/requireTests with tolerant substring-OR-regex matching, exhaustive (no short-circuit) `missing`, and a {pass,missing,ran} return; the implementation stage's GREEN condition is correctly widened to `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` with the missing list injected into the next implementer retry under a "Deliverables still missing" block; and the spec schema (SpecPhase/PhaseDeliverables), `normalizePhases` widening, and `buildSpecPrompt` elicitation are all in place and backward-compatible (no deliverables → early {pass:true}). Typecheck is strict-clean and 53 new tests pass. AND-semantics is sound and well-tested for requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains (which re-read the filesystem each call). However, ONE correctness bug undermines the feature for `requireTests` specifically: the module-level `testListCache` is never invalidated between retry attempts, so a test-list cached on attempt 1 stays stale for attempts 2–3 — meaning once `requireTests` fails, no amount of implementer work can flip the verdict (the new test exists on disk but isn't in the cached list), causing the phase to abort after MAX_ATTEMPTS and produce a false-negative that defeats the very retry mechanism the feature was built to enable. There is also a coverage gap against AC-04 (the combined real-checker × stub-green-gate regression is asserted only with a mocked primitive, not the real `runDeliverableCheck`). Neither is Critical (no silent data corruption / security exposure — the failure is noisy and logged), but the cache staleness is a real production correctness regression, so the verdict is Changes Requested.

findings.title: testListCache is never invalidated, so requireTests cannot recover across retry attempts within a phase (defeats the core retry mechanism)
findings.detail: `testListCache` (module-level Map, build-runner.ts:1629) is populated on the first `loadTestList(cwd,...)` call and returned verbatim on every subsequent call for the same cwd — there is NO `.delete`/`.clear` anywhere (grep confirms only `.get`/`.set`), and the implementation stage never resets it. SCENARIO-009 explicitly tests that the lister spawns once across two calls sharing a cwd, which is correct for the cross-PHASE case — but it is fatal for the cross-ATTEMPT case. Production scenario: a phase declares `requireTests:["my_test"]`. Attempt 1: the implementer omits the test; `runDeliverableCheck` spawns `cargo test -- --list` (or `vitest list --json`), the list lacks `my_test`, requireTests FAILS, the list is cached, and `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these / - missing test: my_test` is injected. Attempt 2: the implementer creates the test on disk; but `loadTestList` returns the STALE cached list (pre-creation), so requireTests STILL reports `missing test: my_test` and the phase stays not-green. Attempt 3: same → the phase aborts after MAX_ATTEMPTS and `allGreen=false; break` terminates the ENTIRE run — even though the test now exists and would pass. This is a false-NEGATIVE (opposite of the false-positive the feature exists to fix) and it specifically breaks the documented recovery path for the most common real-world deliverable ('add a named test'). Note the asymmetry: requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains re-read the filesystem every attempt (correct), but requireTests does not. FIX: invalidate the cache between attempts — e.g. export `clearTestListCache()` (or `clearDeliverableCaches()`) and call it at the top of each attempt iteration in implementation.ts, OR clear it whenever a deliverable check reports any `missing test:` entry so the next attempt re-lists. Re-listing per failed attempt is correct even though `cargo test --list` compiles, because correctness must outrank the redundant-spawn optimization. Confidence: 0.85.

findings.title: AC-04 combined regression is exercised only with a MOCKED runDeliverableCheck, not the real primitive (spec's implementation-deliverable-regression.test.ts is absent)
findings.detail: Acceptance criterion #4 asks for a single regression proving: a phase declaring requireFiles+requireContains+requireNotContains+requireTests, with deliverables ABSENT and a stub build-gate reporting gate.pass===true, yields `runDeliverableCheck(...).pass===false` AND a combined NOT-green verdict. The actual tests split this: tests/build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts proves the real primitive returns pass===false for missing deliverables (in isolation), and tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts (SCENARIO-011/015) proves the AND-semantics verdict composition — but with runDeliverableCheck fully stubbed via vi.mock (it never executes the real regex/fs/spawn logic). The spec's testing strategy named `tests/implementation-deliverable-regression.test.ts`, which does not exist. Risk: an integration defect (e.g. a shape mismatch between the real DeliverableCheckResult and what the verdict line reads) could pass both suites while breaking the end-to-end guarantee the AC was written to lock in. FIX: add one test that imports the REAL runDeliverableCheck (un-mocked) over a temp cwd with the stockfan-shaped absent deliverables, asserts pass===false, and feeds that real result through the verdict line to assert NOT-green. Confidence: 0.6.

findings.title: loadTestList caches by raw cwd, not resolve()'d to absolute — inconsistent with sibling cargoMetadataCache (cache-key/argv skew risk)
findings.detail: loadTestList uses `testListCache.get(cwd)` / `.set(cwd, res)` with the caller-supplied cwd verbatim, whereas the neighboring `loadCargoMetadata` deliberately does `const absCwd = resolve(cwd)` and keys the cache by the absolute path (with an explicit code comment warning about relative/symlinked cwd causing duplicate spawns). In practice implementation.ts always passes an absolute `setup.worktreePath`, so today this only risks a redundant spawn under unusual relative/symlink inputs — not a correctness failure. But it violates the module's own stated cache discipline and is a latent footgun if a future caller passes a relative path. FIX: resolve cwd to absolute once in loadTestList and use it for both the cache key and the spawn (mirroring loadCargoMetadata). Confidence: 0.8.

findings.title: runDeliverableCheck spawns the test-lister even when the build gate already failed (wasted compile on a broken build)
findings.detail: implementation.ts runs `runDeliverableCheck` unconditionally after `runBuildGate`, so when the build is broken (gate.pass===false, e.g. a compile error), the requireTests path still triggers a `cargo test -- --list` — which for cargo is a full compile + list, repeating the just-failed build's cost (mitigated only by the once-per-run cache and the timeout envelope). It is correctness-safe (runDeliverableCheck never throws; a broken build just yields tests:unavailable or a stale-but-harmless list, and existence/grep sub-checks still run) and is consistent with the spec's 'no short-circuit / exhaustive missing' design, so this is an efficiency note, not a defect. Optional optimization: skip the test-list spawn when `!gate.pass && !gate.inScopePass`, since a not-green gate means the phase will retry regardless of the deliverable verdict. Confidence: 0.7.

result.text: Now the tdd-rust, deliverable-wiring, and deliverable-wiring-edges mocks (they already have runDeliverableCheck, need resetDeliverableCheckCache):

I have decisive evidence. Both `npm run typecheck` and the new tests **fail**: `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` is called at `implementation.ts:180` but absent from the import at line 14 (it IS exported at `build-runner.ts:1665`). The module mocks can't rescue it — a free variable the source never imports is a `ReferenceError` regardless of mock, so 6 tests in `implementation.test.ts` fail too. Compiling my findings now.

control.title: Adversarial Review: Build-Gate Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions
control.summary:
The implementation adds a never-throwing `runDeliverableCheck` primitive (Layer 1), widens the schema/normalizer/prompt to carry optional per-phase `deliverables` (Layer 2), AND-s it into the implementation stage's GREEN verdict (Layer 3), and ships a stockfan regression test (Layer 4). The design intent — making GREEN mean "compiles AND deliverables exist", the proven root cause of the 2026-07-20 stockfan false-green — is sound: requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains/requireTests with tolerant matching, a cached test-list spawn, exhaustive (no-short-circuit) `missing`, and a never-throw contract. The requireNotContains semantics were even hardened mid-implementation (a missing/unreadable target now FAILS rather than silently passes).

HOWEVER, the change CANNOT MERGE as-is. The single load-bearing wiring line is broken: `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` is invoked at src/stages/implementation.ts:180 but is NOT in the import statement at line 14 (it IS exported at build-runner.ts:1665). `npm run typecheck` is RED (`TS2304: Cannot find name 'resetDeliverableCheckCache'`) and the targeted test run is RED (6 failed in implementation.test.ts with `resetDeliverableCheckCache is not defined` — a free variable the source never imports cannot be supplied by a module mock). This directly violates acceptance criteria #5 (typecheck + tests green) AND means the production implementation stage throws ReferenceError on the first phase with deliverables (the call is unconditional). This is a textbook "mock masks the real import" defect that the global test suite would have caught but the Layer-4 gate did not. The fix is one line (add `resetDeliverableCheckCache` to the import) but it is a hard blocker.

Beyond the blocker: the requireTests sub-check — the single most valuable assertion, the one that actually catches "a test was never created" — is also the most fragile: it spawns bare `vitest`/`jest`/`pytest` binaries that are typically NOT on PATH (they live in node_modules/.bin or a venv) and silently degrades to "unavailable" on ENOENT, re-opening the exact false-green class the feature exists to close. The run-boundary reset is enforced only by comment (a global mutable cache + undocumented temporal invariant) and is, ironically, the line that's currently broken. Recommend REJECT until the import is fixed and typecheck+full test suite are green; F2 (requireTests binary resolution) should be fixed or explicitly risk-accepted before this can reliably prevent a recurrence of the stockfan bug.

findings.title: Missing import: resetDeliverableCheckCache undefined → typecheck RED, 6 tests RED, runtime crash
findings.detail: implementation.ts:14 imports `{ runBuildGate, runDeliverableCheck, runRedCheck, type GateOptions, type RedStatus }` from build-runner.ts — `resetDeliverableCheckCache` is ABSENT. Yet line 180 calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache();` unconditionally inside every attempt loop. The function IS exported (build-runner.ts:1665). Consequences, all verified: (1) `npm run typecheck` → `src/stages/implementation.ts(180,5): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'resetDeliverableCheckCache'`. (2) `npx vitest run tests/build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts src/stages/implementation.test.ts` → 6 failed in implementation.test.ts with `resetDeliverableCheckCache is not defined` at line 180. The module mocks added in implementation.test.ts / implementation-tdd-rust-wiring.test.ts (`resetDeliverableCheckCache: () => {}`) CANNOT rescue this: vi.mock supplies named exports of the mocked module, but it cannot create a binding for an identifier the source never imported — `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` is a free variable → ReferenceError. (3) Production: the implementation stage throws on the first phase processed (the call is unconditional, not deliverable-gated), outside any try/catch, aborting the run. This violates AC-05 (npm test green) and AC-06 (npm run typecheck green) — the two acceptance criteria whose entire purpose is to catch exactly this. Fix: add `resetDeliverableCheckCache` to the line-14 import. This is a one-liner but is a hard merge blocker; the feature is non-functional as committed.

findings.title: requireTests spawns bare vitest/jest/pytest binaries → ENOENT → silent 'unavailable' → missing tests not caught
findings.detail: resolveTestListerArgv returns bare binaries: `["vitest", "list", "--json"]`, `["jest", "--listTests"]`, `["pytest", "--collect-only", "-q"]`. In real/CI worktrees these are rarely on PATH — they live in node_modules/.bin (vitest/jest) or a venv bin (pytest). spawnSync then yields an ENOENT error → loadTestList records `{available:false}` → runDeliverableCheck pushes `tests:unavailable` and does NOT block, so a declared requireTest that was never created is silently NOT reported missing. This is precisely the stockfan Phase-6 failure mode ('create e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs test') that the feature is meant to prevent, and requireTests — the one sub-check that can catch a never-created test — is the most fragile path. runBuildGate/runRedCheck avoid this because they run via `npm test` / `cargo` (resolved by npm/rustup); the lister bypasses that resolution. Fix: resolve via `node_modules/.bin` (join cwd + that dir, PATH-prepend) or `npx --no-install vitest ...`, and for python resolve the venv interpreter; fall back to `npm test -- --list` for the node family. Without this, requireTests is effectively dead for most repos and the AND-semantics guarantee is undercut exactly where it matters most.

findings.title: Global test-list cache with comment-only run-boundary contract (and the honoring call site is the broken F1 line)
findings.detail: testListCache is a module-global Map. Correctness of the retry mechanism (a freshly-added test on attempt N must be seen) now DEPENDS on every caller of runDeliverableCheck remembering to call resetDeliverableCheckCache() first. This invariant is documented in comments but is NOT enforced by the type system — any future caller (a new stage, a future test, the worktree-router multi-worktree path) that omits the reset gets silent stale-test-list false-negatives. The coupling is also poorly scoped: reset clears ALL cwd entries, not just the current worktree's. Better design: thread the cache through DeliverableCheckOptions (caller owns lifetime) or scope it per-call so a caller physically cannot forget. Compounding irony: the ONE site supposed to honor this contract is implementation.ts:180 — exactly the missing-import line in F1 — so right now the reset NEVER runs at all, meaning even after F1 is fixed, correctness rests entirely on this undocumented global-state invariant.

findings.title: skipTests:!buildGreen defers requireTests on build-red attempts → incomplete feedback to implementer
findings.detail: The optimization `skipTests: !buildGreen` (defensible: avoid wasted compile + poisoned cache on a broken build) means that during a build-RED attempt the requireTests sub-check is skipped, so its missing-test entries are absent from deliverableCheck.missing. That missing list is the ONLY signal fed into the next attempt's '## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these' block. Net: on a build-red attempt the implementer is told 'fix the build' but NOT 'also you need to add test T'. If the implementer fixes the build without adding T, the next attempt is build-green → requireTests runs → missing test surfaces, costing an extra full retry iteration (each iteration is a full agent round + gate). It converges, so not a correctness bug, but it is a latency/incomplete-feedback cost on the highest-value deliverable type. Consider: when skipTests is set, still synthesize 'pending: test <name> will be verified once build is green' hints into the implementer prompt from the declared requireTests.

findings.title: requireNotContains FAILS on a missing/unreadable target file — surprising for spec authors who'd delete-then-recreate
findings.detail: The hardened requireNotContains semantics (a missing/unreadable target file now FAILS rather than silently passing) are internally consistent and defensible — a contract forbidding a pattern in <file> cannot be verified when <file> is absent. But it is a footgun for spec authors: 'requireNotContains screen.rs -> fetch_fmp' reads as 'assert fetch_fmp is gone from screen.rs', and deleting screen.rs (or replacing it) is often a legitimate way to remove that call. The author would get a confusing `missing file: screen.rs` failure. The spec-prompt instruction added in prompts.ts documents requireContains/requireNotContains but does NOT warn that the file must still exist. Recommend either (a) documenting the 'file must still exist' contract in the spec prompt, or (b) making requireNotContains a pure absence check that passes when the file is gone. Pick one and document it.

findings.title: Deliverables shape declared 2-3× (PhaseDeliverables / DeliverableContract / NormalizedPhase.deliverables)
findings.detail: Three near-identical type declarations exist for the optional-deliverables shape: PhaseDeliverables (schemas.ts), DeliverableContract (build-runner.ts:1587), and NormalizedPhase.deliverables: PhaseDeliverables (doc-validators.ts). PhaseDeliverables and DeliverableContract are structurally identical but separately authored. Adding a field (e.g. requireLines, requireMinCount) requires touching ≥2 files in lockstep; drift between them is silent (TS structural typing won't flag a missing optional field). Unify on a single exported type (build-runner imports PhaseDeliverables from schemas, or schemas re-exports DeliverableContract).

findings.title: Untrusted-input vectors: no regex timeout + no path-traversal guard on file checks
findings.detail: (1) tolerantMatch compiles spec-declared patterns via `new RegExp(pattern)` and `.test()` against file contents with NO timeout. A catastrophic-backtracking pattern hangs the (single-threaded) deliverable check; the spawn timeout does not cover in-process regex. The spec is LLM-generated and influenceable via task input / mid-run input injection (a known threat in this repo per learned.md). (2) readForDeliverable resolves `join(cwd, file)` with no traversal guard — a spec with `file: "../../../etc/passwd"` is readable. Contents are NOT exfiltrated into output (only `missing pattern <pat> in <file>` / `unreadable: <path>`), so leak is limited to path-existence oracle, but the read itself crosses the worktree boundary. Mitigations: bound regex eval (or reject patterns over N chars / with nested quantifiers), and reject/normalize `file` paths that escape cwd.

findings.title: `ran` audit array built unconditionally on every check, consumed by one log line
findings.detail: DeliverableCheckResult.ran accumulates a human-readable token per sub-check element on EVERY invocation (file:..., contains:..., not-contains:..., tests:list/unavailable) and is surfaced only in a single ctx.log line in the implementation stage. For a phase with many requireContains entries this is a per-element string push on the hot path of every attempt. Value is marginal (logs already print pass/missing). Consider building ran only at debug/trace level, or dropping it from the return type in favor of structured logging inside the primitive. Negligible perf cost; flagged as a token/clarity minimalism nit.

findings.title: Heavy meta-commentary ('review finding:', SCENARIO-NNN) inflates a now-1936-line hot file
findings.detail: The new ~370 lines carry dense JSDoc that cites review-process chatter ('review finding: ...', 'SCENARIO-007', 'SCENARIO-009') inline. These annotations document the review history rather than future-intent and bloat build-runner.ts — which is loaded into agent context — at ~75 chars/comment-line of marginal maintenance value. Trim to WHY/WHAT; move scenario-ID cross-refs to the test file (they already exist there) or a CHANGELOG note. Keeps the file navigable and reduces context token cost.

result.text: This is decisive. I've found a **Critical blocker**: `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` is called in `implementation.ts:180` but never imported, which breaks `npm run typecheck` AND throws a `ReferenceError` that fails all 10 Phase-3 wiring tests. Let me record the full review.

control.title: Code Review — Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate (spec-10)
control.summary: The implementation is architecturally sound and the never-throwing `runDeliverableCheck` primitive (Layer 1) is well-built and well-tested — the AND-semantics logic, missing-list injection into the next implementer retry, `skipTests`-when-build-failed optimization, per-attempt cache reset, schema/normalizer/prompt elicitation (Layer 2), and backward-compat no-op path are all correct in logic. HOWEVER the change cannot be approved because it FAILS the two hard acceptance gates it explicitly owes (AC-07): `npm run typecheck` exits 2 with `TS2304: Cannot find name 'resetDeliverableCheckCache'`, and `tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts` fails all 10 cases with a runtime `ReferenceError: resetDeliverableCheckCache is not defined`. The root cause is a one-line miss: `implementation.ts` calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` (line 180) but the import on line 14 omits it, AND the `vi.mock("../src/build-runner.ts")` factories in BOTH Phase-3 wiring test files only export `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`/`runDeliverableCheck` — so the function is undefined at runtime under the mock. This is a trivial fix (3 spots) but it is a build-breaker that falsifies the implementation summary's "typecheck + tests green" claim, so the verdict is Blocked. Secondary findings: the `requireTests` tolerant substring match is matched against the ENTIRE raw test-list stdout (cargo headers, file paths, vitest JSON), which risks false-positive matches (i.e. reintroducing the false-green class this feature exists to kill); there is no dedicated end-to-end stockfan regression test exercising the REAL checker (AC-06 is covered only via mocked-checker verdict composition + the primitive unit tests); and the spec prompt emits a redundant top-level `deliverables` bullet that may elicit a misplaced field. Dimension scores — Correctness 2/5 (red build/tests), Security 4/5, Performance 4/5, Concurrency 4/5, Maintainability 4/5, Testability 3/5, Error Handling 5/5, Data Integrity 4/5, Observability 4/5.

findings.title: resetDeliverableCheckCache is called but never imported — typecheck fails (TS2304) and all 10 Phase-3 wiring tests throw ReferenceError
findings.detail: AC-07 requires `npm run typecheck` strict-clean and `npm test` green; both are RED. `src/stages/implementation.ts:180` calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` (added for the per-attempt test-list cache reset), but the import at `implementation.ts:14` is `{ runBuildGate, runDeliverableCheck, runRedCheck, type GateOptions, type RedStatus }` — `resetDeliverableCheckCache` is missing. Result: `npx tsc --noEmit` => `src/stages/implementation.ts(180,5): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'resetDeliverableCheckCache'` (exit 2). At runtime the wiring tests also crash: `tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts` fails 10/10 with `ReferenceError: resetDeliverableCheckCache is not defined` at `implementation.ts:180:5`, because the `vi.mock("../src/build-runner.ts")` factory only exports runRedCheck/runBuildGate/runDeliverableCheck and does not supply `resetDeliverableCheckCache`. This falsifies the implementation summary's claim that typecheck + tests are green, and means the core AND-semantics wiring (AC-03) is UNVERIFIED — every wiring scenario (SCENARIO-011..015) errors before asserting. Failure scenario: CI/pipeline gate rejects the PR; if forced through, every multi-attempt implementation phase crashes on the first deliverable-check attempt. Suggested fix: (1) `implementation.ts:14` — add `resetDeliverableCheckCache` to the named imports from `../build-runner.ts`; (2) in BOTH `tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts` and `tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring-edges.test.ts` add `resetDeliverableCheckCache: () => {}` (or `vi.fn()`) to the `vi.mock` factory object. Confidence 1.0 — reproduced locally (tsc exit 2; vitest 10/10 fail).

findings.title: requireTests tolerant substring match against whole raw test-list stdout risks false-positive (false-green) matches
findings.detail: `tolerantMatch(name, list.list)` matches each declared `requireTests` name as a substring (or regex) against the ENTIRE raw stdout of the test lister. For cargo `test -- --list` that stdout includes binary headers (`Running target/debug/deps/<crate>-<hash>`), file paths, module prefixes, and `[ignored]` tags; for `vitest list --json` it is the full JSON including `file`/`path` fields. A declared name like `"screen"` would PASS if ANY test anywhere (even an unrelated module) contains that substring, or if the string appears in a path/header. This is the dangerous direction for a deliverability gate: a false-positive requireTests match yields a false-green phase — precisely the class of bug this feature is built to eliminate (the stockfan false-green). The substring-OR-regex rule is documented as tolerant-by-design, but nothing anchors the match to a real test-name token. Failure scenario: spec author declares `requireTests:["screen_fallback"]`, the implementer never adds it, but the list already contains `screen_fallback_init` or a path containing `screen_fallback` → phase goes GREEN without the declared test. Suggested fix: for the vitest path, parse the JSON and match against `name`/`fullName` fields only; for cargo, match against lines trimmed of the ` ... [ignored]` suffix and require the match to be a full module-path token (e.g. split on `::`/whitespace and require an element equal to or ending with the declared name). At minimum, prominently document that declared names should be full test identifiers, not fragments.

findings.title: AC-06 stockfan regression test exercises a MOCKED checker, not the real runDeliverableCheck against temp files
findings.detail: AC-04/AC-06 asks for a regression test 'simulating the stockfan Phase-5/6 false-green' proving `deliverableCheck.pass===false` with deliverables ABSENT even given a green stub build-gate, AND `pass===true` when present. The actual coverage splits the proof: `build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts` proves the PRIMITIVE catches missing files/patterns/tests, and `implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts` proves the VERDICT COMPOSITION (`(gate.pass||gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`) by feeding a CANNED `DELIVERABLE_FAIL` through a fully-mocked `runDeliverableCheck`. Neither test runs the REAL checker against the exact stockfan shape (requireFiles:[X] + requireContains:[screen.rs→fetch_us_data] + requireNotContains:[screen.rs→with_retry\(\|\| fetch_fmp] + requireTests:[T]) in both the absent and present directions. So the integration that 'wires the contract end-to-end through the real checker' is asserted only by composition, not by execution. Failure scenario: a future refactor of runDeliverableCheck's file/regex semantics could pass both suites while silently mis-detecting the stockfan shape. Suggested fix: add `tests/implementation-deliverable-regression.test.ts` that, against a real temp worktree, calls `runDeliverableCheck` with the exact stockfan contract in absent-then-present states (this also survives F-01's mock-layer gap).

findings.title: skipTests:true suppresses requireTests from the missing-list across build-failing retries
findings.detail: When the build gate is not green, `skipTests: !buildGreen` skips the requireTests sub-check entirely (intended to avoid spawning the test-lister against a broken build and poisoning the cache — a good optimization). A side effect: `missingDeliverables` will NEVER contain a `missing test: <name>` entry while the build is failing, so the implementer retry prompt's `## Deliverables still missing` block never surfaces the test requirement until the build passes. If the build fails for all MAX_ATTEMPTS(3), the phase fails anyway on the build error (no false-green), so this is acceptable behavior — but it can mask the true root cause (a missing named test) in the audit log and can consume an extra attempt. Suggested fix: none required; add a one-line note in the existing comment that requireTests reasons are deferred until build-green so the behavior is explicit.

findings.title: Spec prompt emits a redundant top-level 'deliverables' bullet that may elicit a misplaced field
findings.detail: In `buildSpecPrompt` the `phases` bullet now reads `{ name, description, deliverables? }` (correct — deliverables is per-phase), but a SEPARATE top-level bullet `- deliverables (optional, per phase): ...` is also added to the 'Data to return' list. An LLM could interpret the second bullet as a sibling top-level key (i.e. emit `deliverables` alongside `phases` rather than inside each phase). The typebox schema only accepts `phases[].deliverables`, so a misplaced top-level `deliverables` would be dropped/ignored on validation depending on `additionalProperties` handling — silently losing the contract rather than failing loudly. Suggested fix: drop the standalone top-level bullet and fold its instruction into the `phases` bullet (e.g. `phases: array of { name, description, deliverables? } where deliverables = {requireFiles, requireContains, requireNotContains, requireTests} declared when a phase's deliverable is not compiler-checkable ...`), or rename the bullet to `phases[].deliverables (optional) ...` to remove ambiguity.

result.text: All gates pass cleanly: build GREEN, typecheck strict-clean, 1173/1173 tests across 68 files.

control.summary:
Fixed the failing build/test/typecheck gate and one medium correctness finding with minimal, targeted changes.

CRITICAL FIX (gate-blocking, TS2304 + ReferenceError): added `resetDeliverableCheckCache` to the `import { ... } from "../build-runner.ts"` line in `src/stages/implementation.ts`. The run-boundary cache-reset call existed (line 180) but the export was never imported, so `tsc` emitted TS2304 and the stage threw ReferenceError at runtime, turning all 10 Phase-3 wiring tests RED. This was the sole cause of the build/typecheck/test failures.

REGRESSION FIX (surfaced by the import fix): the two wiring test files (`tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring.test.ts`, `tests/implementation-deliverable-wiring-edges.test.ts`) mocked `../src/build-runner.ts` without a `resetDeliverableCheckCache` mock; once the real import was wired, the mocked module returned `undefined` and the stage threw. Added `resetDeliverableCheckCache: () => {}` to both mocks.

MEDIUM FIX (requireTests false-positive risk): in `src/build-runner.ts`, the `requireTests` arm of `runDeliverableCheck` now matches each declared test name per-LINE of the cached test-list stdout instead of against the whole raw blob, so a name substring hit in a path/dir-header/comment line can no longer false-green the contract. Verified non-regressing against every SCENARIO-005/006 fixture (cargo/pytest one-per-line; vitest single-line JSON unaffected).

Gate result: npm run build ✓, npm run typecheck ✓ (strict-clean), npm run test ✓ — 1173/1173 tests pass across 68 files (14.2s), including deliverable-check, spec-declaration, wiring, wiring-edges, tdd-rust, stockfan regression, npm-inscope, themed-stream, mid-run-input, and dashboard suites.

Deferred (low priority, non-blocking): skipTests-on-build-red, regex-timeout/path-traversal hardening, unconditional `ran` audit array, redundant deliverables shape declarations, spec-prompt top-level deliverables bullet, and the AC-06 stockfan test using a mocked checker (the real primitive is independently covered by 26 tests in build-runner-deliverable-check.test.ts).

Updated docs/specifications/10-build-gate-deliverable-assertions/10-implementation-summary.md with a detailed "Fix round" section documenting the root cause, each change, and the deferred findings.

result.text:
I have enough evidence. All 1173 tests pass, typecheck clean. Let me record the adversarial findings.

Key confirmations from exploration:
- `runDeliverableCheck` is correct, never-throwing, AND-semantics wired at line 194 (`(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`).
- `loadTestList` is called **once** per `runDeliverableCheck` invocation, and the sole production caller (`implementation.ts:180`) calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` immediately **before** every invocation — so the process-global `testListCache` never serves a hit. Confirmed dead complexity.
- `resolve(cwd, p)` accepts `../` paths with no clamping to the worktree.
- `tolerantMatch` compiles spec-supplied patterns via `new RegExp(pattern).test()` with no timeout.

control.title: Adversarial Review — Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions (build-gate AND-semantics)
control.summary:
The implementation correctly delivers the stated intent: `runDeliverableCheck(cwd, deliverables, opts)` enforces requireFiles/requireContains/requireNotContains/requireTests with tolerant matching, never throws (entire body wrapped in try/catch, SCENARIO-010), and the GREEN condition in implementation.ts:194 is exactly `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass` — the proven root-cause fix for the stockfan false-green. Backward compatibility holds (absent deliverables → `{pass:true}` early return), the spec prompt elicits `phases[].deliverables`, the schema/normalizer round-trip the field as a typed `phase.deliverables`, missing deliverables are fed into the next attempt under the required heading, and `skipTests:!buildGreen` avoids spawning a test-lister on a broken build. `npm run typecheck` is strict-clean and all 1173 tests pass (53 new). The never-throwing invariant, AND-semantics, and the regression test (SCENARIO-011 + the stockfan-shaped wiring tests) are sound.

However, three medium-severity quality concerns warrant an author response before this is considered production-hardened: (1) the test-list cache is architecturally dead weight — ~60 lines of machinery (a process-global Map, a exported reset hook, a sentinel type, resolve()-key normalization, an entire SCENARIO-009 dedupe test) that can never serve a hit because the only caller resets it before every invocation and `loadTestList` runs exactly once per invocation; (2) the MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 budget is shared between build-fix and deliverable-fix retries, so a phase that burns two attempts on the build has only one left to satisfy its deliverable contract — premature termination while still making progress; (3) deliverable paths are resolved with bare `resolve(cwd, p)`, accepting `../` escapes that let a contract be satisfied by a file outside the worktree. None of these risk production failure, data loss, or a security breach (the feature is fail-closed and read-only), so this is CONTEST, not REJECT.

findings.title: Dead test-list cache — ~60 lines of complexity that can never serve a hit
findings.detail: src/build-runner.ts:1639-1664 defines `testListCache` (a process-global `Map<string, TestListResult>`), `resetDeliverableCheckCache()`, the `TestListResult` sentinel type, and `resolve()`-based key normalization, plus ~40 lines of rationale ('SCENARIO-009 dedupe', 'run-boundary reset'). This machinery provides ZERO observable benefit: (a) `loadTestList` (build-runner.ts:1899 region) is called exactly ONCE per `runDeliverableCheck` invocation, so there is nothing to dedup within a call; (b) the only production caller, implementation.ts:180-185, calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` immediately BEFORE every `runDeliverableCheck`, so the cache is always cold when populated and is wiped before any cross-call read. The SCENARIO-009 test ('two requireTests-bearing phases sharing a cwd share one spawn') asserts behavior that the production wiring immediately defeats. The cache's comments even claim it 'dedupes a single runDeliverableCheck call's sub-checks' — vacuously true because there is only one sub-check call. Recommendation: delete `testListCache`, `resetDeliverableCheckCache`, the `TestListResult` sentinel, the `resolve()` keying, and the SCENARIO-009 cross-call dedupe test; let `loadTestList` return its result as a plain local. The reset-before-every-call is itself correct (a retry adds a test, so a stale cache would false-negative) — which is precisely why the cache can never safely dedup and should not exist. This is the single biggest over-engineering surface in the change.

findings.title: Shared MAX_ATTEMPTS budget between build-fix and deliverable-fix retries causes premature termination
findings.detail: src/stages/implementation.ts:103 reuses the single `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` loop (line 16) for BOTH build-gate failures AND deliverable-contract failures. A phase that needs two attempts to get the build green has only ONE attempt left to satisfy `requireFiles`/`requireContains`/`requireTests` — and if the implementer's first deliverable-bearing attempt still misses (e.g. creates the file but mis-wires the call site), the phase reports `failed after 3 attempts — terminating early` (implementation.ts:206) and aborts the entire run (`allGreen=false; break`) even though it was demonstrably converging. The task spec says 'MAX_ATTEMPTS respected' so this is compliant, but the coupling of two independent failure modes into one budget is a robustness regression vs. the pre-deliverable world, where 3 attempts were spent entirely on build/test. Recommendation: either give deliverable-fix attempts a separate small budget (e.g. deliverable failures don't count against build-fix attempts, or a `MAX_DELIVERABLE_ATTEMPTS` constant), or reset the attempt counter when the failure MODE switches (build-fail → deliverable-fail). At minimum, document that the budget is shared.

findings.title: Path escape — deliverable paths resolve outside the worktree with no clamping
findings.detail:
src/build-runner.ts:1844-1854 (requireFiles) and 1856-1876 (requireContains) and 1884-1897 (requireNotContains) all resolve spec-supplied paths with bare `resolve(cwd, p)` / `join(cwd, file)`. A spec declaring `requireFiles: ["../../../../etc/hostname"]` or `requireContains: {file:"../../sibling/preexisting.rs", pattern:"fn main"}` is satisfied by a file OUTSIDE the worktree — defeating the deliverable contract the feature exists to enforce. `requireContains` additionally READS the out-of-cwd file's contents (no data leaks into logs — only pattern names appear in `missing`/`ran` — so this is not an exfiltration vector, but it is a contract-evasion vector). Since the framework's stated job is to ENFORCE contracts rather than trust the spec author, an unguarded path escape cuts against the feature's intent. Recommendation: clamp resolved paths to the worktree root (`resolve(cwd, p)` must startWith `resolve(cwd)`), and record `path escapes worktree:

` as a missing/failure when it does not. Low severity because exploitation requires a malicious or careless spec author, but the whole feature premise is that spec authors' deliverable claims are NOT trusted.

findings.title: Regex ReDoS surface — spec-supplied patterns compiled and run with no timeout
findings.detail: src/build-runner.ts:1699-1714 `tolerantMatch` does `new RegExp(pattern)` then `re.test(text)` synchronously on spec/LLM-supplied patterns. A catastrophic-backtracking pattern (e.g. `(a+)+$`) from a buggy or adversarial spec will hang the Node event loop with no timeout — the `spawnSync({timeout})` envelope only bounds the test-lister subprocess, NOT the in-process regex engine. Because patterns are LLM-generated and never validated, this is a plausible denial-of-pipeline vector, and it would manifest as a silent stage hang rather than a clean failure (the try/catch cannot catch an infinite regex loop). Recommendation: either sandbox the regex in a worker_thread with a hard timeout, reject patterns matching known-dangerous shapes (nested quantifiers), or set a Node-level limit. Low/UNCERTAIN severity because trigger requires a pathological pattern, but the blast radius (whole pipeline hang, no clean failure) is disproportionate.

findings.title: Substring fallback for requireTests cuts toward the exact false-green this feature targets
findings.detail: src/build-runner.ts:1899-1912 matches each declared test name with `lines.some((line) => tolerantMatch(name, line))`, and `tolerantMatch` falls back to `text.includes(pattern)` on any non-matching/invalid regex. A declared `requireTests: ["fetch"]` is therefore satisfied by ANY pre-existing test whose listed name contains 'fetch' — including unrelated tests that pre-date the phase. The per-LINE split (added per an inline review note) mitigates the worst whole-stdout false-hit, and the task explicitly requests tolerant matching, but the tolerance direction is noteworthy: it biases toward the SAME false-green failure mode (a name that superficially matches but is not the deliverable test) that the feature exists to prevent. Recommendation: default to a stricter match (e.g. `\bname\b` or anchoring to the lister's name delimiter) and only fall back to bare substring on an explicitly-declared `loose:true`; or document in the spec-prompt instruction that declared names should be sufficiently specific (the current instruction does not).

findings.title: requireNotContains fail-closed on a MISSING file is stricter than the task spec
findings.detail: src/build-runner.ts:1884-1897 treats a missing target file for `requireNotContains` as a FAILURE (`missing file: <file>`), with an inline comment justifying the choice. The task spec only mandated `unreadable → fail`; it was silent on missing files for requireNotContains, and a naive reading ('a missing file cannot contain the forbidden pattern → pass') would be equally defensible. The chosen fail-closed semantics are arguably MORE correct for the stockfan case (a missing screen.rs means the phase didn't deliver) and align with the anti-false-green intent, so this is not a bug — but it IS an undocumented deviation from the written spec that a future reader could trip on (e.g. a spec author who forbids a pattern in a file that is conditionally generated will see unexpected failures). Recommendation: either add a one-line note to the spec-prompt instruction describing the fail-closed-on-missing semantics for requireNotContains, or accept the deviation explicitly in the contract docstring.

findings.title: Pervasive 'review finding:' comment annotations bloat the module with resolved-iteration narration
findings.detail: src/build-runner.ts carries multiple multi-line 'Review finding:'/'review finding, HIGH'/SCENARIO-XXX comment blocks (e.g. 1613-1621, 1639-1664, 1877-1883, 1899-1912) that narrate review iterations the code has already resolved. While individually informative, in aggregate they add substantial noise at rest (several 10-15 line blocks) and conflate historical review process with present behavior — a maintainer reading the code cold cannot tell which 'review finding' notes still carry actionable weight vs. which are fossil records. The implementation.ts:178-186 block has the same character. Recommendation: distill each 'review finding' block to the one-line invariant it establishes (e.g. 'Reset cache before each attempt: a retry may add a test the stale list would miss') and drop the iteration narrative. Low severity — readability only, no behavioral impact.

result.text: I have sufficient evidence. The implementation is complete, well-tested (typecheck clean, 1173/1173 tests green including all new suites and the critical theme/red-check guards), backward-compatible, never-throwing, and faithfully implements the mandated AND-semantics. Let me synthesize the review.

control.title: Code Review: Per-Phase Deliverable Assertions for the Build Gate
control.summary:
The implementation faithfully realizes the spec's root-cause fix: phase GREEN now requires `(gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) && deliverableCheck.pass`, so a build-green phase that delivers nothing (a never-created test file, an unwired call site, a dead `_ => {}` router arm) is correctly reported as FAIL. Verified on worktree branch `10-build-gate-deliverable-assertions`.

LAYER 1 — `runDeliverableCheck` in `src/build-runner.ts` (lines 1587–1925): sibling of `runRedCheck`/`runBuildGate`, reuses `detectProjectCommands`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, a `readForDeliverable` helper, ONE cached `spawnSync` test-list per cwd (`testListCache` keyed by `resolve(cwd)`), tolerant substring-OR-regex matching (`tolerantMatch`), exhaustive (no short-circuit) evaluation, and a whole-body try/catch honoring the never-throw gate-primitive invariant. Early-returns `{pass:true}` when deliverables is absent (backward compat). All sub-checks produce the exact documented `missing` reason strings.

LAYER 2 — schema/normalizer/prompt: `PhaseDeliverables`/`SpecPhase` added to `schemas.ts` (all fields Optional → backward compatible), `normalizePhases` widened to `NormalizedPhase[]` preserving `deliverables` by reference (round-trips to typed `phase.deliverables`), and `buildSpecPrompt` now instructs declaring deliverables AND-ed with build-green. Clean, minimal diffs.

LAYER 3 — `implementation.ts`: minimal diff — declares `missingDeliverables`, calls `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` + `runDeliverableCheck` after the gate, flips the GREEN condition, and feeds the missing list into the next attempt under `## Deliverables still missing — create/wire these`. MAX_ATTEMPTS (3) loop unchanged.

Evidence: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0 (strict-clean). `npx vitest run` → 68 files / 1173 tests, all green (no regression to runRedCheck, npm-in-scope, cargo gate, themed stream, real-Theme parity, mid-run input). New suites are non-vacuous: the stockfan regression proves AND-semantics both ways (SCENARIO-011/015 + an inScopePass-branch case), and SCENARIO-007/008/009/010 cover no-runner-skip, chmod-000 unreadable (root-skipped), single-cached-spawn, and never-throw.

Two reviewer-driven improvements beyond the spec were already folded in and are correct: a `skipTests` opt (defers the test-lister spawn when the build already failed — the phase fails on the build anyway, so the green verdict is unaffected) and a run-boundary `resetDeliverableCheckCache()` (prevents a stale list from masking a freshly-added test on retry).

No Critical or High issues found. Findings are all Low/Informational. APPROVED.

Dimensions (1–5): Correctness 5, Security 4, Performance 4, Concurrency 5, Maintainability 5, Testability 5, Error Handling 5, Data Integrity 5, Observability 4.

findings.title: requireNotContains on a missing/unreadable target file now FAILs — correct, but the implicit 'file must exist' contract is undocumented
findings.detail: Spec text only specifies the happy path ('requireNotContains hit → forbidden pattern <pattern> still present in <file>'). The implementation goes further: when the target file is missing it records `missing file: <file>`, and when unreadable it records `unreadable: <file>` (build-runner.ts ~1895–1905). This is the SAFER and correct choice for the documented use case (a file that must still exist but must no longer contain a pattern, e.g. stockfan `screen.rs`), and it correctly prevents the false-green where requireNotContains silently passed on an absent file. However, it also means a deliverable expressed as 'delete file X' would misfire (a deleted file is 'missing file:' → FAIL when the intent was satisfied). Fix: add one line to the spec-prompt elicitation and/or a code comment stating 'requireNotContains implies the file must still EXIST; to assert deletion, use requireFiles/requireContains on a replacement file instead.' No code change required.

findings.title: Malformed (non-array) deliverables fields are silently skipped, relying solely on upstream schema validation
findings.detail: `runDeliverableCheck` guards each sub-check with `Array.isArray(...)`; a deliverables object like `{ requireFiles: "x.rs" }` (string instead of array, or a typo like `requireFile`) is silently treated as 'nothing to check' for that sub-check rather than enforced or warned (build-runner.ts ~1845/1865/1893). The intended defense is the typebox `PhaseDeliverables` schema rejecting malformed control upstream, which is the established contract for all fields. This is acceptable, but as defense-in-depth for the false-green class this change targets, consider recording a `missing` reason (e.g. `malformed deliverables.<field>: expected array`) when a deliverables object is present but a declared field is the wrong shape, so a malformed declaration can never silently degrade to pass. Low risk because schema validation is the real gate.

findings.title: Catastrophic-backtracking regex (LLM/spec-supplied) is NOT bounded by resolveTimeoutMs and can stall the gate
findings.detail: `tolerantMatch` runs `new RegExp(pattern).test(text)` in-process (build-runner.ts ~1748). `resolveTimeoutMs` only bounds the spawned test-lister, NOT these regex evaluations. A pathological pattern (e.g. `(a+)+$`) supplied by the LLM-generated spec against a large source file can backtrack indefinitely and hang runDeliverableCheck's main thread — and the never-throw try/catch does NOT help, because regex backtracking does not throw. Realistic risk is low (patterns are dev/LLM-authored, source files are bounded), but since this is exactly the 'gate that stalls the pipeline' failure class the never-throw invariant exists to prevent, it is worth noting. Mitigation options (pick none/one): cap input length before test, run pattern.compile in a try and reject obviously-pathological patterns, or document the threat-model assumption. No action required to approve.

findings.title: requireTests per-line matching assumes vitest `list --json` is single-line; fragile to future pretty-printed output
findings.detail: To avoid false-greens from name substrings appearing in path/comment lines, the requireTests match is performed per-LINE (build-runner.ts ~1917). The code comment explicitly notes vitest `list --json` emits a single-line JSON array 'unaffected' by per-line splitting. This holds today, but if a future vitest version pretty-prints the JSON (multi-line), a test name located on a field-value line would still substring-match (fine), while a name spanning key/value structure would not. Consider falling back to a whole-blob match specifically for the vitest/JSON runner (detected via `resolveTestListerArgv`), or pin/document the vitest-output assumption. Informational — current behavior is correct.

findings.title: TOCTOU window between existsSync and readFileSync in readForDeliverable (single-process, mitigated)
findings.detail: `readForDeliverable` calls `existsSync` then `readFileSync` (build-runner.ts ~1725). If the file is deleted between the two calls, the catch returns `{ok:false, exists:true}` → labelled `unreadable: <file>` when the file is actually missing. The phase still FAILS (correct outcome), only the message is slightly inaccurate. This is a local single-process dev tool, not a concurrent server, so the race is theoretical. No action required.
